LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. (SEE CODE CIV. PROC. 397(C).) DEFENDANTS SHALL REMIT ALL REQUIRED TRANSFER FEES AND COSTS FORTHWITH. ORDER SIGNED. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-19-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-04-2020 Set For Trial On Mar-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant The Hearst Corporation, Hearst Holdings, Inc., William Flemion To Change Venue (Stipulated Continuance, Continued From 3/30/20, Email Dated 5/18/20)
Off Calendar Jun-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHELE HAYDEL GEHRKE of Reed Smith LLP (Tel: 415-659-4798) appearing via CourtCall for moving defendants;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS THE HEARST CORPORATION, HEARST HOLDINGS, INC., AND WILLIAM FLEMION'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:
Defendants The Hearst Corporation, Hearst Holdings, Inc., and Bill Flemion's motion to change venue to San Luis Obispo based upon the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice is granted. (See Code Civ. Proc. 397(c).) Defendants shall remit all required transfer fees and costs forthwith.

The convenience of witnesses and ends of justice mandate the transfer of this action to San Luis Obispo. This case involves an approximately 30-year campaign of alleged harassment and bigotry that occurred on a ranch in San Luis Obispo County. Plaintiff never worked nor lived in San Francisco. (Doherty Decl., par. 6.) It would be inconvenient for numerous non-party witnesses that have probative evidence regarding the issues in this case to travel to San Francisco for the trial of this matter. (See Decls. of Karen Flemion, Sophie Flemion, Byron Haanen, Adam Seagle, Kris Woods Davis, and Angel Estrada.) Plaintiff's demonstration that some of the defendants are headquartered in San Francisco and corporate handbooks emanated from there does not overcome defendants' robust showing that San Luis Obispo is the most convenient forum.

Defendants' motion is timely. A motion to change venue based on the convenience of witnesses must be made within a reasonable time after the answer is filed. (Code Civ. Proc. 396b(d).) Here, defendants answered the amended complaint on January 31, 2020 and filed the instant motion on March 6, 2020. There was no undue delay.

The broad venue provisions of FEHA do not preclude this motion and San Luis Obispo is the best venue given that all of the key events occurred there and all or nearly of all of the potential witnesses live or work there. (See Richfield Hotel Mgmt., Inc. v. Super. Ct. (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 222, 225-227 [holding that venue statute of FEHA does not preclude a change of venue for the convenience of witnesses and that defendant was entitled to a change of venue where it showed that all the potential witnesses lived or worked in Tulare County, where all of the relevant events allegedly took place, and many of the lay witnesses were restaurant employees for whom the lost working time and travel expenses to come to San Mateo County would be significant].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2020 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants Hearst Corporation, Hearst Holdings, Inc. and Bill Flemion's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled. The FAC's allegations more than suffice to (1) plead a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress and (2) avoid any workers' compensation bar. (See Fretland v. County of Humboldt (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1478, 1492.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants Hearst Corporation, Hearst Holdings, Inc. and Bill Flemion's motion to strike is denied. This is merely the pleading stage and the first amended complaint indicates that the continuing violations doctrine may apply. (See Richards v. CH2M Hill, Inc. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 798, 824.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
